title: Set up a SOAP integration from WSDL to test call
question: How do you implement a SOAP integration in ServiceNow from WSDL import through testing?
summary: Walkthrough for SOAP-based integration setup and validation.
scenario: A legacy system only exposes SOAP endpoints and the team needs to call it from ServiceNow.
exactAnswer: Import the WSDL, configure the SOAP message and method, define authentication and parameters, test the request, then parse and validate the response before wiring it into production logic.
steps: Import the WSDL or create the SOAP message record manually.
Review the generated methods and endpoint details.
Use the built-in test related link to send a sample request.
Validate the XML response and downstream field mapping.
validationChecklist: Response values map cleanly into ServiceNow logic.
commonMistakes: Skipping response validation after a successful transport call.

title: Design a SOAP fallback for a hybrid integration
question: How would you design a ServiceNow integration when REST is preferred but a SOAP fallback is still required?
summary: Explains a maintainable hybrid integration pattern.
scenario: Most partner systems support REST, but one region still relies on SOAP and the business wants a unified design.
exactAnswer: Abstract the integration behind a Script Include or spoke-style wrapper so the caller uses one interface while the implementation chooses REST or SOAP per target, then centralize authentication, logging, and error handling.
steps: Define one integration interface for the calling logic.
Implement transport-specific wrappers for REST and SOAP.
Centralize auth, logging, and retry behavior.
Select the transport based on system configuration.
Test both paths with the same business input.
validationChecklist: Callers do not need to know the transport type.
Both transport paths are testable and logged.
commonMistakes: Hardcoding SOAP logic directly in business rules or flows.

title: Validate a new data source before an import
question: What is your implementation path for onboarding a new data source?
summary: Practical data-source validation steps before production imports.
scenario: A team provides a new file or endpoint and wants the data loaded into ServiceNow quickly.
exactAnswer: Validate the data source format, create the import definition, run a controlled import set, inspect the import run details, and only then promote the transform into scheduled production use.
navigationPath: All > System Import Sets > Data Sources
All > Import Sets > Import Set Runs
steps: Load a sample payload or file.
Run a test import to create an import set.
Review the import run details and row issues.
Only then attach or update the transform logic.
validationChecklist: The import set creates records as expected.
Bad rows are understood before scheduling the import.
commonMistakes: Scheduling a source before a controlled test import.
